name: Adam Clayton Powell
abstract: Adam Clayton Powell (May 5, 1865 – June 12, 1953) was an American pastor who developed the Abyssinian Baptist Church in Harlem, New York, as the largest Protestant congregation in the country, with 10,000 members. He was an African American community activist, author, and the father of Congressman Adam Clayton Powell Jr.

concerts.label: St. Mark's Church: Program of New York's Greatest
Concert and Meeting: Joint Organization Movement

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I was there when Senior was there, but then he didn’t stay there that long. He retired. Then Adam, Jr., came in. I mostly remember Adam, Jr., for the years I went there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
